title: Integrations Page
content:
## Integrations Page (`app/dashboard/integrations/page.tsx`) This page displays and manages repository integrations for DocMed. It allows users to connect pairs of repositories where one serves as the source for documentation and the other as the target for updated documentation. ### Purpose - To provide a centralized view of all connected repository pairs. - To allow users to add new repository integrations. - To display any configuration errors encountered during the fetching of integrations. - To guide users on how to add their first integration if none exist. ### Key Components & Logic - **`auth()` from `@clerk/nextjs/server`**: Authenticates the current user to associate integrations with their account. - **`listRepoPairs(userId)` from `@/lib/repo-pairs`**: An asynchronous function that fetches the list of configured repository pairs for the given `userId`. It's wrapped in a `try...catch` block to handle potential errors during fetching. - **`RepoPairCard`**: A component (presumably defined in `./_components/RepoPairCard`) that visually represents a single repository pair, likely displaying details and actions related to that integration. - **`AddPairButton`**: A component (presumably defined in `./_components/AddPairButton`) that triggers the process of adding a new repository integration. - **Error Handling**: Displays a yellow-themed warning banner if `listRepoPairs` throws an error, showing the error message. - **Empty State**: If no repository pairs are found and there are no configuration errors, it displays a "No repository pairs yet." message with a `GitBranch` icon to prompt the user to add an integration. - **Integration List**: If repository pairs are found, it maps over the `pairs` array and renders a `RepoPairCard` for each pair. ### Usage Example This page is rendered as part of the application's dashboard. When a user navigates to `/dashboard/integrations`, this component will: 1. Fetch the authenticated user's ID. 2. Attempt to fetch the user's repository pairs. 3. Render the list of `RepoPairCard` components if successful. 4. Display an error message if fetching fails. 5. Show an onboarding message if no integrations are configured. ```tsx // Example of how this page component might be used within the Next.js App Router // This is an internal page and not intended for direct import elsewhere. import IntegrationsPage from '@/app/dashboard/integrations/page'; // ... in another component or layout file function DashboardLayout() { return (
{/* Other layout elements */} {/* Other layout elements */}
); } ```
